column_settings.title: Column visibility
column_settings.hint: Toggle which columns to display. Preferences are stored in this browser only.

message.confirm_submit: Submit for approval? You cannot edit it afterwards.
message.ai_demo_mode: AI is running in demo mode. Admins can configure real LLM models.

error.login_failed: Login failed. Check your username and password.
error.network_error: Network error. Please try again later.

dashboard.tracker_window_empty: No planned or actual payments in this window

rfq.title: RFQ
rfq.auto_award_content: Auto-select lowest price per item ({{count}} items)
rfq.title_help: Use a title buyers and invited suppliers can recognize quickly, such as project name + item type + time window.
rfq.deadline_help: Set the latest date suppliers should respond. Leave buffer time for comparison and approval before purchase.
rfq.notes_help: Summarize delivery expectations, bidding rules, or special constraints suppliers should know before quoting.
rfq.items_help: List the requested items with clear quantity and specification so suppliers can quote against the same scope.
rfq.suppliers_help: Invite at least one supplier. Add multiple suppliers when you want side-by-side quote comparison.

supplier.title: Suppliers
supplier.name: Name
supplier.payee_name_help: Used as the recipient name on payments; defaults to supplier name if blank
supplier.payee_fallback: Not set — will default to supplier name "{{name}}"
supplier.payee_empty_hint: No payee bank info yet. Add payee name, bank, and account number via Edit Supplier.

shipment.attachment_hint: Upload serial number lists, delivery notes, signing receipts, etc.

pr.items_required: Please add at least one item before submitting
pr.convert_preview_title: This requisition will create {{count}} purchase orders (split by supplier)
pr.items_missing_supplier: Some line items have no supplier assigned and cannot be converted to PO
pr.guide_title_field: Title: briefly describe what you need
pr.guide_required_fields: Company / Cost Center / Expense Type: required for finance
pr.guide_items: Line items: select item and qty, system shows reference prices
pr.guide_price: Price & supplier: not required — buyer handles after approval
pr.guide_reason: Business reason: explain why, approver will see this
pr.price_optional: Optional · decided by buyer after quotation
pr.incomplete_items_warning: Some items lack supplier or price, please supplement before converting to PO
pr.title_help: Use a concise title that lets approvers understand what is being purchased and for which project or team.
pr.company_help: Choose the legal entity that will place the order and own the budget or asset after purchase.
pr.cost_center_help: Pick the cost center that should bear the spend so finance and approval routing stay accurate.
pr.expense_type_help: Choose the accounting nature of the request, such as CapEx or OpEx, based on how finance should book it.
pr.category_help: Optional but recommended. Selecting a procurement category improves reporting and later price analysis.
pr.business_reason_help: Explain why the purchase is needed, who will use it, and any timing or business impact approvers should know.
pr.line_items_help: Add one line per requested item. If the final supplier or price is not fixed yet, submit the item, quantity, and specification first.

system_params.title: System Parameters
system_params.select_company: Select default company (leave empty to auto-pick the only enabled company)
system_params.select_department: Select default department (optional, admins may assign later)
system_params.company_code_not_found: The specified company code does not exist or is disabled
system_params.department_code_not_found: The specified department code does not exist or is disabled

document_template.intro_body: Manage Word or Excel templates for business documents. Use [placeholder] markers in your template to indicate fields to be filled (e.g. [PO Number], [Payee Name], [Amount in Words]). The system will intelligently fill these placeholders using data from the PO, contract, and payment schedule when a user triggers generation.
document_template.syntax_body: Wrap field descriptions in square brackets inside your Word or Excel template, e.g. [PO Number], [Payment Date YYYY-MM-DD]. Date format tokens YYYY/MM/DD are replaced with the installment's actual/planned date. Placeholders containing '大写' output the Chinese uppercase amount (e.g. '肆佰伍拾万元整').
document_template.syntax_examples: Examples: [PO编号] · [合同编号] · [供应商名称] · [收款单位名称] · [收款单位开户行] · [银行账号] · [本期付款金额(大写)] · [付款日期 YYYY年MM月DD日]
document_template.name: Template Name
document_template.filename_template_help: Output filename pattern, also supports [placeholders]. e.g. FinancePaymentForm_[PO Number]_[Installment]_[Payment Date YYYYMMDD]
document_template.placeholders_hint: These are the placeholders the system detected in your template (including filename format); they will be substituted on generation.

admin.recycle_bin_hint: Items deleted from master data appear here. You can restore them to make them active again.
admin.openai_guide_body: When using any OpenAI-compatible third-party service (DeepSeek / GLM / Modelverse, etc.):
admin.openai_guide_provider: Set Provider to openai (or openai-compatible, deepseek, etc.)
admin.openai_guide_model: Model String = vendor's model id — backend auto-prepends openai/ prefix
admin.provider_help: Use openai for any OpenAI-compatible service (or deepseek, modelverse, etc.)
admin.model_string_help: Vendor's model id (e.g. zai-org/glm-4.7); keep existing prefix if present
admin.model_name_help: Use a clear internal name so administrators can quickly recognize this model in routing, tests, and audit logs.
admin.api_base_help: Fill this when the vendor uses an OpenAI-compatible endpoint outside the default provider host, usually ending with /v1.
admin.modality_help: Choose the primary capability this model is meant to serve: text, vision, OCR, or embeddings.
admin.timeout_help: Set a practical request timeout. Long reasoning models usually need more time than simple extraction tasks.
admin.priority_help: Higher-priority active models are preferred first when multiple models can serve the same feature.
admin.routing_enable_confirm_desc: Enabling will call the configured LLM API for this feature and may incur token costs. If the deterministic fallback already covers your needs, you can keep it disabled.
admin.rule_name_help: Give the rule a business-readable name so admins can tell similar amount ranges apart later.
admin.biz_type_help: Choose which business flow this rule governs. Each business type can maintain its own approval ladder.
admin.min_amount_help: Use 0 for the first range when the rule should cover small requests from the bottom upward.
admin.max_amount_help: Leave empty when this rule should cover all larger amounts above the minimum.
admin.stages_editor_help: Define the approval stages in execution order. Each stage routes to one role and the next stage starts only after the previous one completes.
admin.stage_name_help: Use a clear business label shown to administrators and future reviewers, such as Department Approval or Procurement Review.
admin.stage_role_help: Choose which role should receive this stage when the rule is matched.
admin.company_code_help: Use a short stable company code for imports, integrations, and future multi-company identification.
admin.company_name_zh_help: Enter the full Chinese display name local users should see in forms and exports.
admin.company_name_en_help: Optional but recommended for bilingual teams, supplier-facing documents, and exports.
admin.import_suppliers_desc: Excel columns: Name (required), Code, Contact, Phone, Email
admin.import_items_desc: Excel columns: Code (req), Name (req), Category, UoM, Spec
admin.import_prices_desc: Excel columns: Item Code (req), Price (req), Date, Supplier, Currency
admin.confirm_delete_user_body: Hard delete is not reversible. If the user already has related records (PR/PO/approvals/audit logs), deletion will fail — disable the account instead.

saml_help.auth.saml.enabled: Turn on SAML sign-in only after the IdP entity ID, SSO URL, and certificate are filled in and validated.
saml_help.auth.saml.idp.entity_id: Paste the ADFS relying-party issuer / entity ID exactly as published by the identity provider.
saml_help.auth.saml.idp.sso_url: Use the IdP sign-in URL that receives AuthnRequest redirects, for example the ADFS /adfs/ls/ endpoint.
saml_help.auth.saml.idp.slo_url: Optional. Fill this only if your IdP supports single logout and you want Mica to initiate it later.
saml_help.auth.saml.idp.x509_cert: Paste the IdP signing certificate in PEM format. Mica uses it to verify signed SAML responses.
saml_help.auth.saml.sp.entity_id: Leave blank to auto-derive from the current deployment URL, or set a stable public metadata URL explicitly.
saml_help.auth.saml.sp.acs_url: Leave blank to auto-derive the assertion consumer service callback URL, or override it for reverse-proxy/public URL scenarios.
saml_help.auth.saml.attr.email: Attribute name carrying the user's email. ADFS commonly uses the WS-Identity emailaddress claim URI.
saml_help.auth.saml.attr.display_name: Attribute name carrying the display name shown inside Mica after login.
saml_help.auth.saml.attr.groups: Attribute name carrying SAML group or role values used for optional automatic role mapping.
saml_help.auth.saml.jit.enabled: When enabled, first-time SAML users are created automatically during login.
saml_help.auth.saml.jit.default_role: Fallback least-privilege role assigned to auto-created SAML users when group mapping is disabled or no rule matches.
saml_help.auth.saml.jit.default_company_code: Company code assigned to auto-created SAML users. In single-company deployments this can usually stay at the seeded default.
saml_help.auth.saml.jit.default_department_code: Optional department code for auto-created SAML users. Leave blank if admins will assign departments later.
saml_help.auth.saml.group_mapping_enabled: Enable automatic role assignment from SAML groups. When disabled, auto-created users keep the default role.

saml_metadata.refresh_tooltip: Fetch IdP metadata from the URL above now and update the signing certificate (for ADFS cert rotation)
saml_metadata.cert_updated: Signing certificate was updated. Next SSO login will use the new certificate.
saml_metadata.cert_unchanged: Signing certificate is already current — no change needed.
saml_metadata.entity_id_mismatch: Metadata entityId differs from current: {{metadata}} vs {{current}}

admin.password_min_length: Password must be at least 8 characters

sku.title: SKU Price Database
sku.trend_empty: Select SKU(s) to compare price trends
sku.no_price_data: No historical price data for selected SKU(s)
sku.record_price_form_help: Record the source, date, supplier, and actual unit price carefully so later benchmarks and anomaly checks stay trustworthy.
sku.item_help: Select the exact SKU/item this price belongs to so benchmark and anomaly calculations stay comparable.
sku.price_help: Record the actual quoted or observed unit price. More than two decimals are unnecessary for normal procurement analysis.
sku.quotation_date_help: Use the date the quote was issued or observed, not the date you typed it into Mica.
sku.supplier_help: Choose the quoting supplier when known. Leave blank only for generic market research data.
sku.source_type_help: Mark whether the number came from manual entry, a supplier quote, an actual PO, or market research.
sku.anomaly_warning: Recorded, but price anomaly detected: {{pct}}% from benchmark
sku.select_sku_placeholder: Select one or more SKUs to compare

item.title: Items Management
item.code_help: Use a stable internal item code. Once the item is referenced by PRs or PO history, avoid changing it casually.
item.name_help: Use the name buyers and requesters will actually search for, not an overly short abbreviation.
item.category_help: Assign a procurement category whenever possible so reporting, search, and later price analysis remain structured.
item.uom_help: Choose the purchasing unit users will fill in on PR and RFQ forms, such as EA, SET, or LICENSE.
item.spec_help: Capture key differentiators: capacity, model, generation, term length, or any detail approvers and suppliers need to distinguish the item.

contract.mismatch_warning: Planned total doesn't match contract total
contract.upload_hint: No scans yet. Upload PDF/OFD/XML/image files — OCR will be applied automatically for full-text search.
contract.no_schedule_hint: No payment schedule. Click "New Payment Schedule" to create one.
contract.trigger_desc_placeholder: e.g. Equipment installation complete / Warranty 12 months
contract.schedule_form_help: Split the contract into realistic payment milestones so finance, procurement, and suppliers share the same execution plan.
contract.installment_label_help: Name the installment by business meaning, such as down payment, delivery payment, acceptance, or warranty retention.
contract.planned_amount_help: Set the expected payment amount for this milestone. The total of all installments should match the contract total.
contract.planned_date_help: Use this for fixed-date payments. Leave it empty for milestone-triggered installments that do not yet have a hard calendar date.
contract.trigger_type_help: Choose what causes the installment to become payable: date, milestone, invoice receipt, or acceptance.
contract.trigger_desc_help: Optional. Add extra context when the trigger needs a human explanation, such as which acceptance event or document is required.
contract.plan_amount_hint: Installment amounts should equal contract total
contract.edit_schedule_help: Change amount, date, or trigger type for this installment. Already-paid or partially-paid installments cannot be edited.
contract.search_placeholder: Search contract title / number / OCR text
contract.contract_number_help: Recommended format ACME{YYYY}{MM}{DD}{SEQ} (e.g. ACME20260425001); custom values are allowed
contract.change_reason_placeholder: Briefly describe this change (written to version history)
contract.confirm_delete_body: This permanently removes the contract, all version history, and any payment schedule items (that have not yet been paid). This action cannot be undone.
contract.confirm_status_body: This change is recorded in the version history, and the contract will no longer be editable after the transition.
contract.no_shipments_hint: No shipments recorded against the linked PO yet.
contract.ocr_disclaimer: OCR text shown here is machine-extracted for search and reference only. The original document remains authoritative.

invoice.download_hint: Download token is one-time. Reopen from list if needed.

po.invoice_file_required: At least one invoice file required
po.shipment_help: Record only the quantities that physically arrived in this batch. For partial deliveries, keep the remaining quantity for later shipments.
po.payment_help: Create a payment record only when the payable amount and expected timing are clear enough for finance follow-up.
po.payment_amount_help: Enter the amount this payment record should cover. It can be a partial payment and does not need to equal the whole PO total.
po.payment_method_help: Choose the method finance will use or has already used, such as bank transfer or check.
po.payment_due_date_help: Use the contract or invoice due date so overdue and cash-flow tracking stay accurate.
po.payment_date_help: Fill this only if the payment has already happened. Leave blank for planned or pending records.
po.payment_ref_help: Optional. Add the bank reference, voucher number, or internal remittance identifier for reconciliation.
po.payment_contract_required: Please select an associated contract first
po.payment_linked_schedule_help: If an installment is selected, executing this payment will automatically mark the installment as paid. Leave blank to record only against the contract.
po.payment_no_contract_title: This PO has no contract yet
po.payment_no_contract_body: By compliance policy, every payment must be linked to a contract. Use "Create Contract" to add one quickly.
po.payment_confirm_delete_body: Only pending payments can be deleted. Reverse confirmed payments first.
po.no_contracts_hint: No contracts linked to this PO yet. Use "Create Contract" above before recording payments.
po.link_existing_contract_help: Link an existing contract to this PO as a secondary association. The contract's original PO remains its primary PO.
po.no_available_contracts: No additional contracts are available to link.
po.unlink_contract_body: This only removes the secondary association from the current PO. The contract's primary PO remains unchanged.
po.invoice_help: Record the supplier's invoice number, date, tax info, and supporting files so later matching and payment review stay traceable.
